<!--
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2025 Mercator Ocean International <https://www.mercator-ocean.eu/>

SPDX-License-Identifier: EUPL-1.2
-->

---
description: "OceanBench is a benchmarking tool to evaluate ocean forecasting systems against reference ocean datasets and observations."
hide-description: true
author: "Mercator Ocean"
jupyter: python3
format:
  html:
    echo: false
    page-layout: full
---

<div style="text-align: left; margin: 0;">
  <img id="main-logo" class="only-light" src="https://minio.dive.edito.eu/project-oceanbench/public/logo/oceanbench-logo-light.png" alt="OceanBench logo" style="width: 950px; max-width: 100%; height: auto; margin-bottom: -20px;">
  <img id="main-logo-dark" class="only-dark" src="https://minio.dive.edito.eu/project-oceanbench/public/logo/oceanbench-logo-dark.png" alt="OceanBench logo" style="width: 950px; max-width: 100%; height: auto; margin-bottom: -20px;">
</div>


# Evaluating ocean forecasting systems

OceanBench is a benchmarking tool to evaluate ocean forecasting systems against reference ocean analysis datasets (such as 2024 [GLORYS reanalysis](https://data.marine.copernicus.eu/product/GLOBAL_MULTIYEAR_PHY_001_030) and [GLO12 analysis](https://data.marine.copernicus.eu/product/GLOBAL_ANALYSISFORECAST_PHY_001_024)) as well as observations.

Evaluating ocean forecast performance is a complex task, as different users have varying priorities.
Some may focus on accurate predictions of currents, while others prioritize sea level or salinity forecasts.
To accommodate these diverse needs, OceanBench provides a comprehensive set of metrics.
Please keep in mind that these metrics capture key aspects of forecast quality rather than the complete picture.

Want to evaluate your system or get involved in OceanBench? Check out the [GitHub project](https://github.com/mercator-ocean/oceanbench/)!

```{python}
#| output: false

import json
from helpers.s3_discovery import load_downloaded_report_versions, report_local_path, reports_by_region
from helpers.notebook_score_parser import get_all_model_scores_from_notebook, METRIC_TITLES, SECTIONS
from helpers.challenger_metadata import CHALLENGERS
from helpers.published_regions import published_region_label, published_region_metadata

report_versions = load_downloaded_report_versions("reports")
if not report_versions:
    raise RuntimeError("No downloaded evaluation report versions were found.")

def _region_label(version_metadata, region_id):
    metadata = version_metadata.get("regions", {}).get(region_id, {})
    if metadata.get("label"):
        return metadata["label"]
    try:
        return published_region_label(region_id)
    except KeyError:
        return region_id.replace("_", " ").title()

def _region_metadata(version_metadata, region_id):
    metadata = version_metadata.get("regions", {}).get(region_id)
    if metadata is not None:
        return metadata
    try:
        return published_region_metadata(region_id)
    except KeyError:
        return {
            "label": _region_label(version_metadata, region_id),
            "description": "",
            "bounds": None,
        }

def _challenger_label(version_metadata, challenger_name):
    metadata = version_metadata.get("challengers", {}).get(challenger_name, {})
    if metadata.get("label"):
        return metadata["label"]
    if challenger_name in CHALLENGERS:
        return CHALLENGERS[challenger_name]["label"]
    return challenger_name

def _load_scores(version_metadata, report):
    local_path = report_local_path("reports", version_metadata, report)
    scores = get_all_model_scores_from_notebook(local_path, report["challenger"])
    if not scores:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to parse scores for {report['challenger']} from {local_path}")
    return {metric_key: score.model_dump() for metric_key, score in scores.items()}

def _build_version_data(version_metadata):
    published_reports = reports_by_region(version_metadata)
    report_lookup = {
        (report["challenger"], report["region"]): report
        for report in version_metadata["reports"]
    }
    region_ids = list(published_reports)
    all_scores = {
        region_id: {
            "display_name": _region_label(version_metadata, region_id),
            "challengers": {
                challenger_name: _load_scores(version_metadata, report_lookup[(challenger_name, region_id)])
                for challenger_name in published_reports[region_id]
            },
            "challenger_names": list(published_reports[region_id]),
        }
        for region_id in region_ids
    }
    return {
        "regions": all_scores,
        "region_order": region_ids,
        "report_links": {
            region_id: {
                challenger_name: report_lookup[(challenger_name, region_id)]["file"].removesuffix(".ipynb") + ".html"
                for challenger_name in published_reports[region_id]
            }
            for region_id in region_ids
        },
        "region_labels": {
            region_id: _region_label(version_metadata, region_id)
            for region_id in region_ids
        },
        "region_metadata": {
            region_id: _region_metadata(version_metadata, region_id)
            for region_id in region_ids
        },
        "challenger_labels": {
            name: _challenger_label(version_metadata, name)
            for region in all_scores.values()
            for name in region["challenger_names"]
        },
    }

version_data = {
    version_metadata["version"]: _build_version_data(version_metadata)
    for version_metadata in report_versions
}
default_version = report_versions[0]["version"]
scores_payload = {
    "versions": [
        {
            "id": version_metadata["version"],
            "label": version_metadata.get("label", version_metadata["version"]),
            "generated_at": version_metadata.get("generated_at"),
            "oceanbench_version": version_metadata.get("oceanbench_version"),
        }
        for version_metadata in report_versions
    ],
    "default_version": default_version,
    "version_data": version_data,
    "metric_titles": METRIC_TITLES,
    "sections": SECTIONS,
}
scores_payload.update(version_data[default_version])
scores_json = json.dumps(scores_payload)
```

```{python}
#| output: asis

print(f'<script type="application/json" id="scores-data">{scores_json}</script>')
```

::: {#all-scores}

## Comparison to observations

Absolute RMSE scores against in-situ observations (Class IV validation). The colors denote % difference to the selected baseline.

<div id="observations-scores"></div>

## Comparison to reanalysis

Absolute RMSE scores against 2024 [GLORYS reanalysis](https://data.marine.copernicus.eu/product/GLOBAL_MULTIYEAR_PHY_001_030). These are the deterministic scores for the forecasted variables and physically consistent diagnostic variables. The colors denote % difference to the selected baseline.

<div id="reanalysis-scores"></div>

## Comparison to analysis

Absolute RMSE scores against 2024 [GLO12 analysis](https://data.marine.copernicus.eu/product/GLOBAL_ANALYSISFORECAST_PHY_001_024). These are the deterministic scores for the forecasted variables and physically consistent diagnostic variables. The colors denote % difference to the selected baseline.

<div id="analysis-scores"></div>

:::

Implemented by [Mercator Ocean](https://mercator-ocean.eu).

<a href="https://mercator-ocean.eu"><img src="https://www.nemo-ocean.eu/wp-content/uploads/MOI.png" alt="Mercator Ocean logo" height="100"/></a>

```{=html}
<script src="vendor/d3-array.v3.min.js"></script>
<script src="vendor/d3-geo.v3.min.js"></script>
<script src="vendor/topojson-client.v3.min.js"></script>
<script src="region-globe.js"></script>
<script src="interactive-scores.js"></script>
```
